headline_summary: Iran war-related incidents expand into Gulf states: drones hit Kuwait’s Mina al-Ahmadi refinery and a power/water plant; U.S. aircraft are downed over Iran/Gulf.
Reuters reports U.S. intelligence sees Iran unlikely to ease its effective Strait of Hormuz blockade soon, sustaining disruption to a key oil chokepoint.
Diplomatic tracks: UK-led talks among ~40 countries explore post-ceasefire coalition options; UN Security Council vote planned on Bahrain-drafted shipping protection resolution with China opposed.
Selective transit/assurances: Philippines says Iran promised safe passage for Philippine-flagged ships; CMA CGM vessel transits Hormuz after signaling 'Owner France'.
Oil market structure reflects acute tightness: Reuters reports record WTI prompt backwardation and elevated prices; OPEC+ weighs further output hike contingent on reopening.
risk_drivers: Effective closure/constraint of the Strait of Hormuz and uncertainty over reopening timeline
Direct kinetic spillover into Gulf infrastructure (refinery and utilities hit in Kuwait)
Air/air-defense incidents involving U.S. aircraft and rescue operations
Potential for Iran to formalize permit/fee regime for transit, keeping insurance/freight elevated
Unclear end-state and ongoing escalation rhetoric

Energy: oil_up_risk: sustained supply shock and higher freight/insurance costs while Hormuz remains constrained
Refined product volatility risk from Gulf refinery disruptions (Kuwait incident)
Backwardation/near-term tightness may persist until credible reopening/escort plan emerges
FX: em_fx_pressure: higher energy import bills pressure EM current accounts
risk_off_flows could support USD and safe havens if Gulf spillover widens
Rates: rates_credit_spreads: higher energy-driven inflation risk can lift term premia while widening credit spreads in risk-off
Event risk may increase rate volatility around geopolitical headlines
Equities: risk_off_flows: broad equity drawdown risk on escalation; relative support for energy/defense vs transports
Supply-chain and fuel cost headwinds for cyclicals

sources.title: Exclusive: US intelligence warns Iran unlikely to ease Hormuz Strait chokehold soon, sources say
Drones hit Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery, fires reported, no injuries
Britain says 40 countries discuss reopening Strait of Hormuz after Iran blockade
UN to vote on Hormuz resolution as China opposes authorization of force
World anxious to open Hormuz Strait while Trump and Iran trade threats
Near-term oil prices hit record premium over later deliveries after Trump vows more attacks on Iran
OPEC+ likely to weigh further oil output hike on Sunday, sources say
